title: Board Abstraction Pattern: Narrow Waist + BSP Adapter (STM32F4)
intended_audience: LLM/maintainer implementing multi-board embedded Rust support (stm32f4xx-hal + optional BSP)

constraints: Maintain support for F412 and F413 without requiring a separate BSP crate.
Support F469 using both HAL and BSP (BSP may be modified).
The implementer is allowed to change both BSP APIs and HAL integration for cleanliness and type unification.
Shared application code should remain board-agnostic.
context.primary_goal: Remove board-specific branching and BSP leakage from shared init by introducing a narrow, stable board interface.

pattern_summary.name: Narrow Waist Board Interface + BSP Adapter
pattern_summary.one_sentence: Expose a small, stable Board API used by the app; implement it per-board (HAL-only for F412/F413, BSP-adapted for F469) so board differences don't leak into shared code.
why_it_helps: Prevents BSP-only concepts (e.g., 'remainders') from leaking into the application
Reduces tuple-return brittleness by standardizing return shape

design_principles: Single board entrypoint: shared code calls one function to obtain a uniform struct of initialized peripherals.
Standard return shape: avoid per-board tuple variations; prefer a named struct.
Board-private complexity: GPIO splitting, peripheral ownership juggling, BSP remainder plumbing must stay inside the board module/BSP.
Trait-first interoperability: prefer returning types that implement embedded-hal and embedded-graphics traits.
Avoid HAL/PAC version skew: ensure BSP and app share the same HAL/PAC types (re-export or unify versions/features).
Keep cfg limited: only board selection should be cfg'd in shared code.

BoardIo.description: Uniform set of initialized peripherals the app depends on.

fields.name: i2c
fields.notes: Board-specific type alias; must satisfy embedded-hal i2c traits used by touch

fields.name: touch
fields.notes: Preferred: ft6x06::Ft6X06 driver or wrapper exposing same methods

fields.name: button
fields.notes: Board-specific type alias; may be HAL pin or BSP wrapper

fields.name: serial
fields.notes: USB serial abstraction used by app

type_aliases.name: I2C
type_aliases.purpose: Concrete I2C peripheral type for the board

type_aliases.name: Button
type_aliases.purpose: Concrete button type for the board

type_aliases.name: DisplayInner
type_aliases.purpose: Concrete display driver or wrapper type

type_aliases.name: TouchInterruptPin
type_aliases.purpose: Concrete touch interrupt pin type if used by touch driver

constants_and_helpers: SCREEN_WIDTH, SCREEN_HEIGHT (and any derived layout constants)
Any board-specific coordinate rotation/axis swap logic

init_entrypoint.name: init_board_io
init_entrypoint.recommended_signature: fn init_board_io(p: pac::Peripherals, cp: pac::CorePeripherals) -> (BoardIo, Rcc, SysDelay)
alternatives: Return only BoardIo and keep clocks/delay in shared init
Return a BoardParts struct including clocks/delay if that's cleaner
init_entrypoint.hard_requirement: The application should be able to initialize a board using one call and receive a uniform BoardIo (or equivalent named struct).

bsp_integration_guidance_for_f469.core_rule: Treat the BSP as an implementation detail hidden behind the same narrow waist API.
do_not_leak: BSP-specific 'remainders' objects or pin ownership artifacts
BSP-only wrapper types that do not exist on HAL-only boards (unless wrapped behind common traits/types)
preferred_shapes: BSP returns stm32f4xx-hal types directly (best), OR
BSP returns wrappers/newtypes that implement the same embedded-hal / embedded-graphics traits used by the app
recommended_bsp_changes: Add bsp::hal and bsp::pac re-exports to avoid version skew
Make BSP init functions return types compatible with the app’s BoardIo fields
Keep remainder plumbing private inside BSP; expose only final peripherals/handles

display: Must support embedded-graphics DrawTarget<Color=Rgb565> and OriginDimensions (or the subset the app uses).
If BSP display type differs, wrap it in a newtype implementing the traits and use that as board::DisplayInner.
i2c: Must implement the embedded-hal I2C traits required by the touch driver.
Prefer using the same i2c driver type across boards (at least trait-compatible).
touch: Preferred: return ft6x06::Ft6X06<...> directly for all boards to avoid divergent APIs.
usb_serial: Either standardize SerialDriver::new signature, or hide USB bus init behind board::init_serial returning SerialDriver.

cfg_policy: Allow cfg only for selecting which board module is active.
Avoid cfg branches inside shared make_devices() for board-specific init; push that into board modules.
return_shapes: Avoid tuples with >3 elements for board init; use named structs (BoardIo/Parts) for clarity and stability.

recommended_refactor_plan.action: Introduce BoardIo (and any BoardParts wrapper) in the shared board abstraction module.
recommended_refactor_plan.success_criteria: A single struct represents all peripherals the app needs from a board.

recommended_refactor_plan.action: Define init_board_io(...) in each board module (f412/f413/f469) returning BoardIo.
recommended_refactor_plan.success_criteria: Shared code can build DeviceContext without per-board branching.

recommended_refactor_plan.action: Move GPIO splitting and init ceremony fully into each board module.
recommended_refactor_plan.success_criteria: Shared init never splits ports or handles board-specific pins directly.

recommended_refactor_plan.action: Adapt/modify F469 BSP so it returns HAL-compatible or trait-compatible types; keep 'remainders' private.
recommended_refactor_plan.success_criteria: f469.rs has minimal glue; no BSP-private objects appear outside the board module.

recommended_refactor_plan.action: Standardize touch initialization: board module creates TouchDriver for all boards.
recommended_refactor_plan.success_criteria: Shared code never handles touch interrupt pins or touch I2C address directly.

recommended_refactor_plan.action: Delete shared cfg branches like #[cfg(not(feature='stm32f469'))] by using the uniform BoardIo.
Add compile-time guards to enforce exactly one board feature.
recommended_refactor_plan.success_criteria: Misconfigured builds fail fast with clear messages.

compile_time_guards.description: Enforce that exactly one board feature is enabled.
snippet: compile_error!("Enable exactly one board feature: stm32f412 | stm32f413 | stm32f469");
compile_error!("Enable only one board feature at a time.");

do: Keep board-specific peripheral ownership juggling inside the board module/BSP.
Return types that implement embedded-hal / embedded-graphics traits used by the app.
Use named structs (BoardIo/Parts) instead of large tuples.
Re-export HAL/PAC from BSP to avoid duplicate types.
Standardize touch/display interfaces across boards via wrappers if needed.
dont: Don’t let BSP-only concepts like 'remainders' leak into shared code.
Don’t branch shared init logic per board beyond selecting the active board module.
Don’t allow BSP and app to depend on different HAL versions/features.
Don’t use divergent APIs for touch/display unless wrapped behind the narrow waist.

acceptance_criteria: Shared DeviceContext/make_devices() compiles unchanged across all three board features (except for module selection).
No BSP-private types appear in shared modules.
Board init returns a single named struct with consistent field names across boards.
F469 remains free to use BSP internally while exporting the same narrow waist API as HAL-only boards.
Adding a new board requires: new board module implementing init_board_io + exports, plus feature wiring—no changes in app logic.

common_pitfalls_and_mitigations.pitfall: HAL/PAC type mismatch between BSP and app
common_pitfalls_and_mitigations.mitigation: Re-export hal/pac from BSP and/or ensure a single dependency instance with identical features/versions.

common_pitfalls_and_mitigations.pitfall: BSP returns wrapper types that don’t implement required traits
common_pitfalls_and_mitigations.mitigation: Add thin newtype adapters implementing embedded-hal/embedded-graphics traits; keep adapters in board module or BSP.

common_pitfalls_and_mitigations.pitfall: Touch init differs (interrupt pin, address, calibration) and leaks into shared code
common_pitfalls_and_mitigations.mitigation: Move all touch setup and calibration into board::init_board_io; return a uniform TouchDriver.

common_pitfalls_and_mitigations.pitfall: Large tuple returns become unmaintainable and error-prone
common_pitfalls_and_mitigations.mitigation: Replace tuples with named structs everywhere at the board boundary.

glossary.narrow_waist: A small, stable interface layer between two complex subsystems (app and board init).
glossary.bsp_adapter: Code that converts BSP-specific initialization outputs into the narrow waist types expected by the app.
glossary.trait_alignment: Ensuring returned objects implement common embedded traits (embedded-hal, embedded-graphics) so higher layers stay generic.
glossary.cfg_sprawl: Excessive conditional compilation branches scattered through shared code.
